headline: Spread model was 10x too pessimistic; HYP-105/106 retracted for lookahead leakage; HYP-107 real but execution unresolved at ~5% gross.
what_happened: Execution harness measured real bid/ask on 59 gapper events: median 0.61% spread vs. model's 8% cap. Backtester charge was -3.49% vs. reality +1.02% — a +4.51pp pessimistic bias, not edge.

HYP-105 (long ramp 9:31–10:30 fade) and HYP-106 (filtered skew) both confirmed OOS then REFUTED: Phase-3 ML leaked intraday push data through the holding window. Signals are real but features aren't forward-tradeable.

HYP-107 reconstructed honestly: 57-event untouched holdout shows gross median +5.4% / 70% win on moderate-gap quiet-opens, but 09:31 microcap spreads (1–15%) + LULD halts likely consume most. Live shadow required to resolve.
Shadow gapper live returned 2 signals on 2026-07-13 (+0.0456%), 1 on 2026-07-14 (+0.0042%), zero the last 3 days. Constitutional day returns negligible; not live edge yet.
Dashboard rebuilt (light theme, skills.html, oracle brief panel live); oracle daily digest JSON 200 / plist loaded.
what_it_means: The backtest bias was systematic pessimism, not discovery of real edge—all historical gapper results improve by 4–5pp once realistic spreads are used. HYP-105/106 were inflated by lookahead; HYP-107's real signal (moderate-gap runners) is raw +5% gross but eaten by execution friction and halts in real microcap conditions. You need live 09:31 shadow data with true fills before sizing anything; shadow returns so far are noise.
todays_one_thing: Deploy live 09:31 shadow with execution harness (bid/ask + halt detector + realistic fill model) on HYP-107's leak-free filter (overnight_gap ≤0.577 AND log10 first-min vol ≤5.854) to measure whether the +5.4% holdout signal survives real spreads and halts. This is the gate to the funded-sim day (G1 1/30).
